task_completion.description: Create a dashboard named 'Insider_Threat_Analytics' in the Search & Reporting app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Web UI showing a dashboard with the exact title 'Insider_Threat_Analytics' within the Search & Reporting app.

task_completion.description: Add a global Time Picker to the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Time Picker input control (e.g., a dropdown showing time ranges like 'Last 24 hours') located at the top of the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Create a panel named 'Concurrent IP Usage'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dashboard panel with the exact title 'Concurrent IP Usage'.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'Concurrent IP Usage' search logic for 15-minute intervals and >= 2 distinct IPs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the search bar, panel edit view, or source code for a query containing `index="security_logs"`, `Accepted password`, a 15-minute time bin (e.g., `bin _time span=15m`), and a distinct count filter for IPs (e.g., `dc(src_ip) >= 2` or `where ip_count >= 2`).
Look for a second dashboard panel with the exact title 'Off-Hours Access'.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'Off-Hours Access' search logic for hours 0 through 4.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the search bar, panel edit view, or source code for a query containing `index="security_logs"`, successful authentications (e.g., `Accepted password`), and an hour filter (e.g., `date_hour>=0 date_hour<=4` or `date_hour<5`).

task_completion.description: Link both panels to the global Time Picker.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the dashboard edit view or source XML to verify the panels' time ranges are set to use the shared Time Picker (often indicated by a link icon in the UI or `$time.earliest$` / `$time.latest$` tokens in the source).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI to build the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Splunk web interface (http://localhost:8000), using the search bar, and clicking dashboard creation buttons, rather than just writing an XML file in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the panels are actual Splunk search-driven panels.
integrity.visual_evidence: The panels should display actual search results (tables, charts, or event lists) or a 'waiting for data' state typical of Splunk searches, not static HTML/Markdown text boxes mocking the expected output.

integrity.description: Verify the agent executed and tested the searches.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Splunk Search view with the query typed in the search bar, a timeline of events, and parsed fields on the left sidebar.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named exactly 'Insider_Threat_Analytics'. The target index for the searches must be 'security_logs'. The Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
